title: Reverse String
description:
Write a function that reverses a string. The input string is given as an array of characters `s`.

You must do this by modifying the input array **in-place** with O(1) extra memory.
inputFormat: A single line containing the string to reverse.
outputFormat: A single line containing the reversed string.
constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 10^5
s[i] is a printable ASCII character.

title: Merge Two Sorted Arrays
description:
You are given two integer arrays `nums1` and `nums2`, sorted in non-decreasing order. Merge `nums2` into `nums1` as one sorted array.

The final sorted array should be printed as space-separated integers.
inputFormat:
The first line contains integers m and n — sizes of the two arrays.
The second line contains m space-separated integers — elements of nums1.
The third line contains n space-separated integers — elements of nums2.
outputFormat: A single line of space-separated integers — the merged sorted array.

title: Binary Search
description:
Given a sorted array of distinct integers `nums` and a target value `target`, return the index if the target is found. If not, return `-1`.

You must write an algorithm with **O(log n)** runtime complexity.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n — the size of the array.
The second line contains n space-separated sorted integers.
The third line contains the target integer.
outputFormat: A single integer — the index of the target, or -1 if not found.
constraints:
1 <= nums.length <= 10^4
-10^4 < nums[i], target < 10^4
All integers in nums are unique.
nums is sorted in ascending order.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description:
Given a linked list represented as a sequence of node values and a position `pos` where the tail connects to (0-indexed, -1 means no cycle), determine if the linked list has a cycle.

Return `true` if there is a cycle, `false` otherwise.
inputFormat:
The first line contains n — the number of nodes.
The second line contains n space-separated integers — the node values.
The third line contains pos — the position the tail connects to (-1 if no cycle).
constraints:
0 <= n <= 10^4
-10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5
pos is -1 or a valid index.

title: Rotate Image
description:
You are given an `n x n` 2D matrix representing an image. Rotate the image by 90 degrees clockwise.

You must rotate it **in-place**.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The next n lines each contain n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: Print the rotated matrix, n lines of n space-separated integers.

title: Course Schedule
description:
There are `numCourses` courses labeled from `0` to `numCourses - 1`. You are given an array of prerequisites where `prerequisites[i] = [a, b]` indicates you must take course `b` before course `a`.

Return `true` if you can finish all courses, `false` otherwise.
inputFormat:
The first line contains two integers: numCourses and number of prerequisites p.
The next p lines each contain two space-separated integers a b.

title: Climbing Stairs
description:
You are climbing a staircase. It takes `n` steps to reach the top.

Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?
inputFormat: A single integer n.
outputFormat: A single integer — the number of distinct ways.

title: Implement Trie
description:
Implement a trie (prefix tree) with the following operations:

- `INSERT word` — Inserts the word into the trie.
- `SEARCH word` — Returns `true` if the word is in the trie.
- `STARTSWITH prefix` — Returns `true` if any word starts with the prefix.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer q — number of operations.
The next q lines each contain an operation.
outputFormat: For each SEARCH and STARTSWITH operation, print the result on a new line.
constraints:
1 <= word.length, prefix.length <= 2000
word and prefix consist of lowercase English letters.
At most 3 * 10^4 operations.
sampleInput:
5
INSERT apple
SEARCH apple
SEARCH app
STARTSWITH app
INSERT app

testCases.input:
5
INSERT apple
SEARCH apple
SEARCH app
STARTSWITH app
INSERT app

3
INSERT hello
SEARCH world
STARTSWITH hel

title: FizzBuzz
description:
Given an integer `n`, return a list of strings from `1` to `n` where:
- divisible by 3 → `Fizz`
- divisible by 5 → `Buzz`
- divisible by both → `FizzBuzz`
- otherwise → the number as a string.
inputFormat: A single integer n.
outputFormat: Print n lines, each with the appropriate output.

testCases.expectedOutput:
1
2
Fizz
4
Buzz
Fizz
7
8
Fizz
Buzz
11
Fizz
13
14
FizzBuzz

title: Palindrome Number
description: Given an integer `x`, return `true` if `x` is a palindrome, and `false` otherwise.
inputFormat: A single integer x.

title: Maximum Subarray
description: Given an integer array `nums`, find the subarray with the largest sum, and return its sum.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n — the size of the array.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: A single integer — the maximum subarray sum.

title: House Robber
description:
You are a robber planning to rob houses along a street. Adjacent houses have security systems — if two adjacent houses are broken into, the police will be alerted.

Return the **maximum amount** you can rob without alerting the police.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: A single integer — the maximum amount.

title: Coin Change
description:
You are given an integer array `coins` representing coins of different denominations and an integer `amount`.

Return the **fewest number of coins** that you need to make up that amount. If that amount cannot be made up, return `-1`.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n — the number of coin types.
The second line contains n space-separated integers — the coin denominations.
The third line contains the target amount.
outputFormat: A single integer — the fewest number of coins, or -1.

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Given an integer array `nums`, return the length of the longest **strictly increasing** subsequence.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: A single integer — the length of the LIS.

title: Number of Islands
description: Given an `m x n` 2D grid map of `1`s (land) and `0`s (water), return the number of islands.
inputFormat:
The first line contains two integers m and n.
The next m lines each contain n characters (0 or 1) space-separated.
outputFormat: A single integer — the number of islands.

title: Word Ladder
description: Given two words, `beginWord` and `endWord`, and a dictionary `wordList`, return the **number of words in the shortest transformation sequence** from `beginWord` to `endWord`, or `0` if no such sequence exists.
inputFormat:
The first line contains the beginWord.
The second line contains the endWord.
The third line contains an integer n.
The next n lines each contain a word.
outputFormat: A single integer — the length of the shortest transformation sequence, or 0.
sampleInput:
hit
cog
6
hot
dot
dog
lot
log
cog

testCases.input:
hit
cog
6
hot
dot
dog
lot
log
cog

hit
cog
5
hot
dot
dog
lot
log

title: Longest Common Prefix
description: Write a function to find the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings. If there is no common prefix, return an empty string.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n — the number of strings.
The next n lines each contain a string.
outputFormat: A single line — the longest common prefix.

title: Roman to Integer
description:
Given a roman numeral string, convert it to an integer.

I=1, V=5, X=10, L=50, C=100, D=500, M=1000.
Subtraction cases: IV=4, IX=9, XL=40, XC=90, CD=400, CM=900.
inputFormat: A single line containing the roman numeral string.
outputFormat: A single integer — the converted value.

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings `strs`, group the anagrams together. An anagram is a word formed by rearranging the letters of another word.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The next n lines each contain a string.
outputFormat: Print each group on a new line, words space-separated, groups sorted alphabetically.
sampleInput:
6
eat
tea
tan
ate
nat
bat
sampleOutput:
ate eat tea
bat
nat tan

testCases.input:
6
eat
tea
tan
ate
nat
bat
testCases.expectedOutput:
ate eat tea
bat
nat tan

title: Product of Array Except Self
description:
Given an integer array `nums`, return an array `answer` such that `answer[i]` is equal to the product of all the elements of `nums` except `nums[i]`.

Solve in O(n) time **without using division**.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: A single line of n space-separated integers.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string `s` containing just the characters `(`, `)`, `{`, `}`, `[` and `]`, determine if the input string is valid.
inputFormat: A single line containing the string of brackets.
constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 10^4
s consists of parentheses only '()[]{}'.

title: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k` most frequent elements.
inputFormat:
The first line contains two integers n and k.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: A single line of k space-separated integers (sorted ascending).
constraints:
1 <= nums.length <= 10^5
k is in the range [1, number of unique elements].

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of `intervals` where `intervals[i] = [start_i, end_i]`, merge all overlapping intervals.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The next n lines each contain two space-separated integers: start and end.
outputFormat: Print each merged interval on a new line.

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
description: Design an algorithm to serialize and deserialize a binary tree. Input is level-order with `null` for missing nodes.
inputFormat: A single line of space-separated values (integers or 'null').
outputFormat: The same level-order traversal after serialize → deserialize round-trip.
constraints:
The number of nodes is in range [0, 10^4].
-1000 <= Node.val <= 1000

title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return the indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.
The third line contains the target integer.
outputFormat: Print two space-separated integers — the indices (0-indexed).
constraints:
2 <= nums.length <= 10^4
-10^9 <= nums[i] <= 10^9
Only one valid answer exists.

title: Matrix Diagonal Sum
description: Given a square matrix `mat`, return the sum of the matrix diagonals. If an element is on both diagonals, count it only once.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The next n lines each contain n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: A single integer — the diagonal sum.

title: Sort Colors
description: Given an array `nums` with n objects colored red (0), white (1), and blue (2), sort them in-place so that objects of the same color are adjacent (Dutch National Flag problem).
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The second line contains n space-separated integers (0, 1, or 2).
outputFormat: A single line of n space-separated sorted integers.
constraints:
n == nums.length
1 <= n <= 300
nums[i] is 0, 1, or 2.

title: Kth Largest Element in Array
description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k-th` largest element in sorted order.
inputFormat:
The first line contains two integers n and k.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: A single integer — the k-th largest element.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Given a sorted integer array that has been rotated at an unknown pivot, find the target value's index or return -1. Must achieve O(log n).
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.
The third line contains the target.
outputFormat: A single integer — the index of target, or -1.

title: Container With Most Water
description: Given `n` vertical lines, find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container with the most water.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The second line contains n space-separated integers — the heights.
outputFormat: A single integer — the maximum area.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree (level-order with `null`), determine if it is a valid BST.
inputFormat: A single line of space-separated values (integers or 'null').

title: LRU Cache
description:
Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache.

Both GET and PUT operations must run in O(1) average time.
inputFormat:
The first line contains two integers: capacity and number of operations q.
The next q lines each contain an operation: either `PUT key value` or `GET key`.
outputFormat: For each GET operation, print the result on a new line.
sampleInput:
2 6
PUT 1 1
PUT 2 2
GET 1
PUT 3 3
GET 2
GET 3

testCases.input:
2 6
PUT 1 1
PUT 2 2
GET 1
PUT 3 3
GET 2
GET 3

title: Reverse a String
description: Reverse the given string in-place.
inputFormat: A single line containing the string to reverse.
outputFormat: A single line containing the reversed string.

title: Merge Sorted Arrays
description: Merge two sorted arrays into a single sorted array.
inputFormat:
The first line contains integers m and n.
The second line contains m integers.
The third line contains n integers.
outputFormat: A single line of merged sorted integers.

title: Sort a Stack
description: Given a stack of integers (top first), sort it using only one temporary stack.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The second line contains n space-separated integers (top first).
outputFormat: A single line of sorted integers (smallest first).

title: Implement Queue Using Stacks
description: Implement a FIFO queue using only two stacks.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer q.
The next q lines each contain an operation.
outputFormat: For each POP, PEEK, EMPTY operation, print the result.
sampleInput:
6
PUSH 1
PUSH 2
PEEK
POP
EMPTY
POP

testCases.input:
6
PUSH 1
PUSH 2
PEEK
POP
EMPTY
POP

title: Shortest Path in Grid
description: Find the length of the shortest path from top-left to bottom-right in an `m x n` grid (0 = empty, 1 = obstacle). Return -1 if no path exists.
inputFormat:
The first line contains two integers m and n.
The next m lines each contain n space-separated integers (0 or 1).
outputFormat: A single integer — the shortest path length, or -1.

title: Kth Largest Element
description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k-th` largest element in sorted order.
inputFormat:
The first line contains two integers n and k.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.

title: Minimum Cost Path
description: Given an `m x n` grid, find a path from top-left to bottom-right which minimizes the sum. Move only down or right.
inputFormat:
The first line contains two integers m and n.
The next m lines each contain n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: A single integer — the minimum path sum.

title: Decode String
description: Given an encoded string `k[encoded_string]`, return the decoded version.
inputFormat: A single line containing the encoded string.
outputFormat: A single line containing the decoded string.

title: Palindrome Partition Count
description: Given a string `s`, return the minimum number of cuts so that every substring is a palindrome.
inputFormat: A single line containing the string s.
outputFormat: A single integer — the minimum number of cuts.

title: Jump Game
description: Given an integer array where each element represents your maximum jump length, return `true` if you can reach the last index.
inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer n.
The second line contains n space-separated integers.

title: Matrix Diagonal Sum [Contest: algo sprint]
description: Return the sum of both diagonals of a square matrix. Center counted once for odd-sized matrices.
inputFormat:
The first line contains n.
The next n lines each contain n integers.

title: Count Primes
description: Return the number of prime numbers strictly less than `n`. Use the Sieve of Eratosthenes.
inputFormat: A single integer n.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: Maximize profit by choosing a single day to buy and a different day to sell. Return 0 if no profit.
inputFormat:
The first line contains n.
The second line contains n prices.
outputFormat: A single integer — the maximum profit.

title: Generate All Subsets
description: Return all possible subsets (power set) of unique elements in lexicographic order.
inputFormat:
The first line contains n.
The second line contains n sorted integers.
outputFormat: Each subset on a new line, space-separated.

title: Reverse Linked List in Groups
description: Reverse the linked list `k` nodes at a time. Leave remaining nodes as-is if less than `k`.
inputFormat:
The first line contains n and k.
The second line contains n values.

title: Two Sum [Contest: weekly 02]
description: Given an array and a target, return the indices of the two numbers that add up to the target.
inputFormat: n, array, target.

title: Group Anagrams [Contest: weekly 02]
description: Group anagrams together from an array of strings.
inputFormat: n and n strings.

6
eat
tea
tan
ate
nat
bat
sampleOutput:
ate eat tea
bat
nat tan

eat
tea
tan
ate
nat
bat

inputFormat: n and array.
